mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate compresses the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month decline in the unemployment rate signals accelerating labor demand and wage growth, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls > 0.5 percentage points in 3 months
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or zero predictive power for 20-day returns in more than 40% of triggered instances across a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and retail sales confirms robust consumer credit availability and spending momentum, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint HOUST and RSAFS strength fails to outperform baseline SPX returns by at least 1.5% over the 60-day forward window in a rolling 3-year test.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during late-cycle expansions reflects inventory drawdowns and capacity utilization peaks, providing a short-term earnings tailwind that lifts equity prices over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month changes show no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns (p > 0.10) over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month inflation acceleration exceeding 0.3 percentage points forces market participants to price in faster monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above the threshold consistently coincide with positive or flat 20-day equity returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous quarterly real GDP growth above 2.5 percent and monthly payroll gains exceeding 150,000 confirm a self-sustaining economic expansion, driving positive equity returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and PAYEMS signal yields a negative or statistically insignificant Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month employment growth continuously reduces recession risk premiums embedded in equity valuations, producing a persistent upward drift in S&P 500 prices over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month changes fail to predict 5-day forward returns with a directional accuracy exceeding 55% over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: During active monetary tightening cycles, declining housing starts serve as a leading indicator of credit contraction and consumer spending weakness, predicting negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: HOUST declines during rate hike periods do not produce negative 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances, or the signal's Sharpe ratio falls below zero.
